<content>1: // ─── Unit Tests: Keep-Awake Management (Ticket 3.5) ─────────────────────────
2: //
3: // Tests:
4: // T1:  Constructor defaults (enabled, not active)
5: // T2:  activate() on macOS spawns caffeinate with -di flags
6: // T3:  activate() emits activated event
7: // T4:  deactivate() kills process, emits deactivated
8: // T5:  isActive() tracks state correctly
9: // T6:  setEnabled(false) deactivates if active
10: // T7:  setEnabled(true) doesn't auto-activate
11: // T8:  Non-macOS: activate() emits unsupported, isActive() false
12: // T9:  Idempotent: double activate doesn't spawn twice
13: // T10: Idempotent: double deactivate is safe
14: // T11: Process exit handler: unexpected exit emits error, resets state
15: // T12: isSupported() returns true only on darwin
16: // T13: Disabled: activate() is no-op when not enabled
17: // T14: PBT: enabled/disabled state machine consistency
18: // T15: activate() when spawn throws synchronously
19: 
20: import type { ChildProcess } from "node:child_process";
21: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
22: import fc from "fast-check";
23: import { describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
24: import { KeepAwake } from "../../src/lib/keep-awake.js";
25: 
26: const SEED = 42;
27: const NUM_RUNS = 100;
28: 
29: // ─── Mock helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30: 
31: /** Create a mock ChildProcess (EventEmitter with kill) */
32: function createMockChild(): ChildProcess & EventEmitter {
33: 	const child = new EventEmitter() as ChildProcess & EventEmitter;
34: 	Object.defineProperty(child, "pid", { value: 12345, writable: true });
35: 	Object.defineProperty(child, "kill", {
36: 		value: vi.fn(() => true),
37: 		writable: true,
38: 	});
39: 	return child;
40: }
41: 
42: /** Create a mock spawn function that returns the given child */
43: function createMockSpawn(child: ChildProcess & EventEmitter) {
44: 	return vi.fn(
45: 		() => child,
46: 	) as unknown as typeof import("node:child_process").spawn;
47: }
48: 
49: // ─── Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50: 
51: describe("Ticket 3.5 — Keep-Awake Management", () => {
52: 	// ─── T1: Constructor defaults ────────────────────────────────────────
53: 
54: 	describe("T1: Constructor defaults", () => {
55: 		it("defaults to enabled", () => {
56: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({ _platform: "darwin" });
57: 			expect(ka.isEnabled()).toBe(true);
58: 		});
59: 
60: 		it("defaults to not active", () => {
61: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({ _platform: "darwin" });
62: 			expect(ka.isActive()).toBe(false);
63: 		});
64: 
65: 		it("is an EventEmitter", () => {
66: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({ _platform: "darwin" });
67: 			expect(typeof ka.on).toBe("function");
68: 			expect(typeof ka.emit).toBe("function");
69: 			expect(typeof ka.removeListener).toBe("function");
70: 		});
71: 
72: 		it("respects enabled: false in constructor", () => {
73: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({ _platform: "darwin", enabled: false });
74: 			expect(ka.isEnabled()).toBe(false);
75: 		});
76: 	});
77: 
78: 	// ─── T2: activate() on macOS spawns caffeinate ───────────────────────
79: 
80: 	describe("T2: activate() on macOS spawns caffeinate with -di flags (AC1)", () => {
81: 		it("spawns caffeinate with correct args", () => {
82: 			const child = createMockChild();
83: 			const mockSpawn = createMockSpawn(child);
84: 
85: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({
86: 				_platform: "darwin",
87: 				_spawn: mockSpawn,
88: 			});
89: 			ka.activate();
90: 
91: 			expect(mockSpawn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
92: 				"caffeinate",
93: 				["-di"],
94: 				expect.objectContaining({
95: 					stdio: "ignore",
96: 					detached: false,
97: 				}),
98: 			);
99: 		});
100: 
101: 		it("spawns with custom command and args", () => {
102: 			const child = createMockChild();
103: 			const mockSpawn = createMockSpawn(child);
104: 
105: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({
106: 				_platform: "darwin",
107: 				_spawn: mockSpawn,
108: 				command: "/usr/bin/caffeinate",
109: 				args: ["-dims"],
110: 			});
111: 			ka.activate();
112: 
113: 			expect(mockSpawn).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
114: 				"/usr/bin/caffeinate",
115: 				["-dims"],
116: 				expect.any(Object),
117: 			);
118: 		});
119: 	});
120: 
121: 	// ─── T3: activate() emits activated event ────────────────────────────
122: 
123: 	describe("T3: activate() emits activated event (AC1)", () => {
124: 		it("emits activated on successful spawn", () => {
125: 			const child = createMockChild();
126: 			const mockSpawn = createMockSpawn(child);
127: 
128: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({
129: 				_platform: "darwin",
130: 				_spawn: mockSpawn,
131: 			});
132: 
133: 			const events: string[] = [];
134: 			ka.on("activated", () => events.push("activated"));
135: 
136: 			ka.activate();
137: 
138: 			expect(events).toEqual(["activated"]);
139: 		});
140: 	});
141: 
142: 	// ─── T4: deactivate() kills process, emits deactivated ──────────────
143: 
144: 	describe("T4: deactivate() kills process, emits deactivated (AC2)", () => {
145: 		it("kills the child process", () => {
146: 			const child = createMockChild();
147: 			const mockSpawn = createMockSpawn(child);
148: 
149: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({
150: 				_platform: "darwin",
151: 				_spawn: mockSpawn,
152: 			});
153: 
154: 			ka.activate();
155: 			ka.deactivate();
156: 
157: 			expect(child.kill).toHaveBeenCalled();
158: 		});
159: 
160: 		it("emits deactivated event", () => {
161: 			const child = createMockChild();
162: 			const mockSpawn = createMockSpawn(child);
163: 
164: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({
165: 				_platform: "darwin",
166: 				_spawn: mockSpawn,
167: 			});
168: 
169: 			const events: string[] = [];
170: 			ka.on("deactivated", () => events.push("deactivated"));
171: 
172: 			ka.activate();
173: 			ka.deactivate();
174: 
175: 			expect(events).toEqual(["deactivated"]);
176: 		});
177: 	});
178: 
179: 	// ─── T5: isActive() tracks state correctly ──────────────────────────
180: 
181: 	describe("T5: isActive() tracks state correctly", () => {
182: 		it("false before activate", () => {
183: 			const ka = new KeepAwake({ _platform: "darwin" });
184: 			expect(ka.isActive()).toBe(false);
185: 		});

<content>1: // ─── Keep-Awake Management (Ticket 3.5) ─────────────────────────────────────
2: // Prevents the host machine from sleeping during long-running agent tasks.
3: // Uses `caffeinate` on macOS, no-op on other platforms.
4: 
5: import type { ChildProcess } from "node:child_process";
6: import { spawn as defaultSpawn } from "node:child_process";
7: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
8: 
9: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10: 
11: export interface KeepAwakeOptions {
12: 	enabled?: boolean;
13: 	command?: string;
14: 	args?: string[];
15: 	/** Injectable platform for testing */
16: 	_platform?: string;
17: 	/** Injectable spawn for testing */
18: 	_spawn?: typeof import("node:child_process").spawn;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface KeepAwakeEvents {
22: 	activated: [];
23: 	deactivated: [];
24: 	error: [{ error: Error }];
25: 	unsupported: [{ platform: string }];
26: }
27: 
28: // ─── Defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29: 
30: const DEFAULT_COMMAND = "caffeinate";
31: const DEFAULT_ARGS = ["-di"];
32: 
33: // ─── KeepAwake ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34: 
35: export class KeepAwake extends EventEmitter {
36: 	private readonly command: string;
37: 	private readonly args: string[];
38: 	private readonly platform: string;
39: 	private readonly spawnFn: typeof import("node:child_process").spawn;
40: 
41: 	private enabled: boolean;
42: 	private child: ChildProcess | null = null;
43: 	private active = false;
44: 
45: 	constructor(options?: KeepAwakeOptions) {
46: 		super();
47: 		this.enabled = options?.enabled ?? true;
48: 		this.command = options?.command ?? DEFAULT_COMMAND;
49: 		this.args = options?.args ?? [...DEFAULT_ARGS];
50: 		this.platform = options?._platform ?? process.platform;
51: 		this.spawnFn = options?._spawn ?? defaultSpawn;
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
55: 
56: 	/** Start keeping awake (spawns caffeinate on macOS) */
57: 	activate(): void {
58: 		// No-op if disabled
59: 		if (!this.enabled) {
60: 			return;
61: 		}
62: 
63: 		// AC5: Idempotent — don't spawn again if already active
64: 		if (this.active) {
65: 			return;
66: 		}
67: 
68: 		// AC4: No-op on non-macOS
69: 		if (this.platform !== "darwin") {
70: 			this.emit("unsupported", { platform: this.platform });
71: 			return;
72: 		}
73: 
74: 		// AC1: Spawn caffeinate
75: 		try {
76: 			const child = this.spawnFn(this.command, this.args, {
77: 				stdio: "ignore",
78: 				detached: false,
79: 			});
80: 
81: 			this.child = child;
82: 			this.active = true;
83: 
84: 			// AC6: Handle unexpected exit
85: 			child.on("exit", (_code, _signal) => {
86: 				// Only treat as error if we didn't initiate the deactivation
87: 				if (this.active) {
88: 					this.active = false;
89: 					this.child = null;
90: 					this.emit("error", {
91: 						error: new Error(`${this.command} exited unexpectedly`),
92: 					});
93: 				}
94: 			});
95: 
96: 			child.on("error", (err: Error) => {
97: 				this.active = false;
98: 				this.child = null;
99: 				this.emit("error", { error: err });
100: 			});
101: 
102: 			this.emit("activated");
103: 		} catch (err) {
104: 			this.active = false;
105: 			this.child = null;
106: 			this.emit("error", {
107: 				error: err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err)),
108: 			});
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	/** Stop keeping awake (kills caffeinate) */
113: 	deactivate(): void {
114: 		// AC5: Idempotent — safe to call multiple times
115: 		if (!this.active || !this.child) {
116: 			return;
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		const child = this.child;
120: 		this.active = false;
121: 		this.child = null;
122: 
123: 		try {
124: 			child.kill();
125: 		} catch {
126: 			// Process may already be dead
127: 		}
128: 
129: 		this.emit("deactivated");
130: 	}
131: 
132: 	/** Is currently keeping awake? */
133: 	isActive(): boolean {
134: 		return this.active;

<content>1: // ─── Storage Monitor (Ticket 6.2 AC8) ───────────────────────────────────────
2: // Periodically checks available disk space and emits events on transitions
3: // between low/ok states. Used by the Daemon to warn about disk space issues.
4: 
5: import { EventEmitter } from "node:events";
6: import { statfs as nodeStatfs } from "node:fs/promises";
7: 
8: // ─── Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export interface StorageMonitorOptions {
11: 	/** Path to check disk space for */
12: 	path: string;
13: 	/** Threshold in bytes below which low_disk_space is emitted (default: 100MB) */
14: 	thresholdBytes?: number;
15: 	/** Polling interval in milliseconds (default: 5 minutes) */
16: 	intervalMs?: number;
17: 	/** Injectable statfs for testing — defaults to wrapping Node.js fs.statfs() */
18: 	_statfs?: (path: string) => Promise<{ available: number }>;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface LowDiskSpaceEvent {
22: 	availableBytes: number;
23: 	thresholdBytes: number;
24: }
25: 
26: export interface DiskSpaceOkEvent {
27: 	availableBytes: number;
28: }
29: 
30: // ─── Defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31: 
32: const DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
33: const DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes
34: 
35: // ─── Default statfs wrapper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: async function defaultStatfs(path: string): Promise<{ available: number }> {
38: 	const stats = await nodeStatfs(path);
39: 	return { available: stats.bavail * stats.bsize };
40: }
41: 
42: // ─── StorageMonitor ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: 
44: export class StorageMonitor extends EventEmitter {
45: 	private readonly monitorPath: string;
46: 	private readonly thresholdBytes: number;
47: 	private readonly intervalMs: number;
48: 	private readonly statfsFn: (path: string) => Promise<{ available: number }>;
49: 
50: 	private timer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
51: 	private wasLow: boolean | null = null; // null = no check yet
52: 	private checking = false;
53: 
54: 	constructor(options: StorageMonitorOptions) {
55: 		super();
56: 		this.monitorPath = options.path;
57: 		this.thresholdBytes = options.thresholdBytes ?? DEFAULT_THRESHOLD_BYTES;
58: 		this.intervalMs = options.intervalMs ?? DEFAULT_INTERVAL_MS;
59: 		this.statfsFn = options._statfs ?? defaultStatfs;
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	// ─── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
63: 
64: 	/** Start periodic polling. First check runs immediately, then on interval. */
65: 	start(): void {
66: 		// Run the first check immediately
67: 		void this.check();
68: 
69: 		// Set up periodic polling
70: 		this.timer = setInterval(() => {
71: 			void this.check();
72: 		}, this.intervalMs);
73: 
74: 		// Don't keep the process alive just for this timer
75: 		this.timer.unref();
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	/** Stop polling (idempotent). */
79: 	stop(): void {
80: 		if (this.timer !== null) {
81: 			clearInterval(this.timer);
82: 			this.timer = null;
83: 		}
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	// ─── Private ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
87: 
88: 	private async check(): Promise<void> {
89: 		if (this.checking) return;
90: 		this.checking = true;
91: 		let available: number;
92: 		try {
93: 			({ available } = await this.statfsFn(this.monitorPath));
94: 		} finally {
95: 			this.checking = false;
96: 		}
97: 		const isLow = available < this.thresholdBytes;
98: 
99: 		if (isLow && this.wasLow !== true) {
100: 			// Transition to low (from ok/unknown)
101: 			this.wasLow = true;
102: 			this.emit("low_disk_space", {
103: 				availableBytes: available,
104: 				thresholdBytes: this.thresholdBytes,
105: 			} satisfies LowDiskSpaceEvent);
106: 		} else if (!isLow && this.wasLow === true) {
107: 			// Transition to ok (from low)
108: 			this.wasLow = false;
109: 			this.emit("disk_space_ok", {
110: 				availableBytes: available,
111: 			} satisfies DiskSpaceOkEvent);
112: 		} else if (!isLow && this.wasLow === null) {
113: 			// First check is ok — just record state, no event
114: 			this.wasLow = false;
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: }
